_publications._publicationTitle: Programming of Schwann Cells by Lats1/2-TAZ/YAP Signaling Drives Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumorigenesis.

_contextSnippet: The Lats1/2-deficient mice carrying three copies of Lats1/2 deletion alleles...developed nerve-associated tumors with 100% penetrance. Tumors arose in the dermis, spinal nerve roots and peripheral nerves
details: Lats1/2-deficient mice developed nerve-associated tumors with 100% penetrance, including dermal low-grade (grade I PNST) and paraspinal/peripheral high-grade (grade III) GEM-PNSTs resembling human MPNSTs
